metadata.name: Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS) Managed Cluster
metadata.description: This module deploys an Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS) Managed Cluster.
metadata.compliance:
Compliant usage of this module requires the following parameter values:

- aadProfile.enableAzureRBAC: true, and enableRBAC: true
- addonAzurePolicy.enabled: true
- apiServerAccessProfile.disableRunCommand: true
- apiServerAccessProfile.enablePrivateCluster: true
- diagnosticSettings: 'workspaceResourceId' is not empty and required 'categories' are provided (see parameter description for more details)
- disableLocalAccounts: true
- enableRBAC: true
- kubernetesVersion: null (default) or one of supported versions [Microsoft Learn](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/aks/supported-kubernetes-versions?tabs=azure-cli)
- securityProfileDefender.securityMonitoring.enabled: true
- publicNetworkAccess: 'Disabled'
- networkOutboundType: not 'loadBalancer'

metadata.description: Required. Should this add-on be enabled?
Optional. Whether to enable rotation of secrets.
Optional. Configuration of the add-on.
Azure Key Vault secrets provider configuration.
Required. Should this add-on be enabled?
Optional. Version of the policy. Default: v2
Optional. Configuration of the add-on.
Required. Whether to enable Azure Defender. Default: true
Required. Security Monitoring settings.

Optional. Resource ID of the Log Analytics workspace to be associated with Microsoft Defender.
When Microsoft Defender is enabled, this field is required and must be a valid workspace resource ID.
When Microsoft Defender is disabled, leave the field empty.
Optional. Whether to enable Azure Key Vault key management service. Default: true

Conditional. Resource ID of key vault. When keyVaultNetworkAccess is Private, this field is required and must be a valid resource ID.
When keyVaultNetworkAccess is Public, leave the field empty.

Optional. Network access of key vault. The possible values are Public and Private.
Public means the key vault allows public access from all networks.
Private means the key vault disables public access and enables private link. The default value is Private.

Conditional. Identifier of Azure Key Vault key.
It is provided as a Uri (e.g. 'https://vaultFQDN/keys/key_name/key_version'). See key identifier format for more details.
When Azure Key Vault key management service is enabled, this field is required and must be a valid key identifier.
When Azure Key Vault key management service is disabled, leave the field empty.
Azure Key Vault key management service settings for the security profile

Optional. IP ranges are specified in CIDR format, e.g. 137.117.106.88/29.
This feature is not compatible with clusters that use Public IP Per Node, or clusters that are using a Basic Load Balancer.
For more information see [API server authorized IP ranges](https://docs.microsoft.com/azure/aks/api-server-authorized-ip-ranges).
Default: empty.
Optional. Whether to disable run command for the cluster or not. Default: true
Optional. For more details, see [Creating a private AKS cluster](https://docs.microsoft.com/azure/aks/private-clusters). Default: true
Optional. Whether to create additional public FQDN for private cluster or not. Default: false

Optional. Allowed values are 'system', 'none', or a resourceId of the private DNS Zone. The default is 'none'.

For more details see [configure private DNS zone](https://docs.microsoft.com/azure/aks/private-clusters#configure-private-dns-zone).

Conditional. Resource ID of the subnet.
It is required when: 1. creating a new cluster with BYO Vnet; 2. updating an existing cluster to enable apiserver vnet integration.
The access profile for managed cluster API server.
Optional. The list of AAD group object IDs that will have admin role of the cluster. Default: empty.
Optional. Specifies whether to enable Azure RBAC for Kubernetes authorization. Default: true.
Optional. Specifies whether to enable managed AAD integration. Default: true.

Optional. Specifies the tenant ID of the Azure Active Directory used by the AKS cluster for authentication.
If not specified, will use the tenant of the deployment subscription.
The Azure Active Directory configuration.

Optional. The desired number of allocated SNAT ports per VM. Allowed values are in the range of 0 to 64000 (inclusive).
The default value is 0 which results in Azure dynamically allocating ports.
Optional. The type of the managed inbound Load Balancer BackendPool. The default is 'NodeIPConfiguration'
Optional. The health probing behavior for External Traffic Policy Cluster services.
Optional. Enable multiple standard load balancers per AKS cluster or not.

Optional. Desired outbound flow idle timeout in minutes. Allowed values are in the range of 4 to 120 (inclusive).
The default value is 4 minutes.

Required. The desired number of IPv4 outbound IPs created/managed by Azure for the cluster load balancer.
Allowed values must be in the range of 1 to 100 (inclusive). The default value is 1.

Optional. The desired number of IPv6 outbound IPs created/managed by Azure for the cluster load balancer.
Allowed values must be in the range of 0 to 100 (inclusive). The default value is 0 for single-stack and 1 for dual-stack.
Optional. Desired managed outbound IPs for the cluster load balancer.
Required. The fully qualified Azure resource id.
Required. A list of public IP prefix resources.
Optional. Desired outbound IP Prefix resources for the cluster load balancer.
Required. The fully qualified Azure resource id.
Required. A list of public IP resources.
Optional. Desired outbound IP resources for the cluster load balancer.
Profile of the cluster load balancer.
Required. Indicates the enablement of Advanced Networking observability functionalities on clusters.
Required. Observability profile to enable advanced network metrics and flow logs with historical contexts.

Advanced Networking profile for enabling observability on a cluster.
Note that enabling advanced networking features may incur additional costs.
For more information see aka.ms/aksadvancednetworking.

Required. The default is Unmanaged, but may change to either NodeImage or SecurityPatch at GA.
See https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/aks/auto-upgrade-cluster?tabs=azure-cli

Required. For more information see setting the AKS cluster auto-upgrade channel
see https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/aks/upgrade-cluster#set-auto-upgrade-channel.
The auto upgrade configuration.
Required. Whether to enable Image Cleaner on AKS cluster.
Required. Image Cleaner scanning interval in hours.  The maximum value is equivalent to three months.
Image Cleaner settings for the security profile.
Required. Type of maintenance configuration - Cluster-level or Node-level.
Required. Configuration parameters.
Required. Indicates if Application Monitoring Auto Instrumentation is enabled or not.

Optional. Application Monitoring Auto Instrumentation for Kubernetes Application Container.
Deploys web hook to auto-instrument Azure Monitor OpenTelemetry based SDKs to collect OpenTelemetry metrics,
logs and traces of the application. See aka.ms/AzureMonitorApplicationMonitoring for an overview.
Optional. Indicates if Application Monitoring Open Telemetry Logs and traces is enabled or not.
Optional. The Open Telemetry host port for Open Telemetry logs and traces. If not specified, the default port is 28331.

Optional. Application Monitoring Open Telemetry Metrics Profile for Kubernetes Application Container Logs and Traces.
Collects OpenTelemetry logs and traces of the application using Azure Monitor OpenTelemetry based SDKs. See aka.ms/AzureMonitorApplicationMonitoring for an overview.
Required. Indicates if Application Monitoring Open Telemetry Metrics is enabled or not.
Optional. The Open Telemetry host port for Open Telemetry metrics. If not specified, the default port is 28333.

Optional. Application Monitoring Open Telemetry Metrics Profile for Kubernetes Application Container Metrics.
Collects OpenTelemetry metrics of the application using Azure Monitor OpenTelemetry based SDKs. See aka.ms/AzureMonitorApplicationMonitoring for an overview.

Optional. Application Monitoring Profile for Kubernetes Application Container. Collects application logs,
metrics and traces through auto-instrumentation of the application using Azure Monitor OpenTelemetry based SDKs.
See aka.ms/AzureMonitorApplicationMonitoring for an overview.

Optional. Indicates whether custom metrics collection has to be disabled or not. If not specified the default is false.
No custom metrics will be emitted if this field is false but the container insights enabled field is false

Optional. Indicates whether prometheus metrics scraping is disabled or not. If not specified the default is false.
No prometheus metrics will be emitted if this field is false but the container insights enabled field is false
Required. Indicates if Azure Monitor Container Insights Logs Addon is enabled or not.
Optional. Fully Qualified ARM Resource Id of Azure Log Analytics Workspace for storing Azure Monitor Container Insights Logs.
Optional. The syslog host port. If not specified, the default port is 28330.

Optional. Azure Monitor Container Insights Profile for Kubernetes Events, Inventory and Container stdout & stderr logs etc.
See aka.ms/AzureMonitorContainerInsights for an overview.
Required. Whether to enable the Prometheus collector
Required. Comma-separated list of additional Kubernetes label keys that will be used in the resource's labels metric.
Required. Comma-separated list of Kubernetes annotations keys that will be used in the resource's labels metric.
Optional. Kube State Metrics for prometheus addon profile for the container service cluster
Optional. Metrics profile for the prometheus service addon
Addon profile for monitoring the container service cluster.
Required. Length of maintenance window range from 4 to 24 hours.
The start date of the date span.
The end date of the date span.

Optional. Date ranges on which upgrade is not allowed. 'utcOffset' applies to this field.
For example, with 'utcOffset: +02:00' and 'dateSpan' being '2022-12-23' to '2023-01-03',
maintenance will be blocked from '2022-12-22 22:00' to '2023-01-03 22:00' in UTC time.
Required. The date of the month.
Required. Specifies the number of months between each set of occurrences.
Optional. For schedules like: 'recur every month on the 15th' or 'recur every 3 months on the 20th'.
Required. Specifies the number of days between each set of occurrences.
Optional. For schedules like: 'recur every day' or 'recur every 3 days'.
Required. Specifies on which day of the week the maintenance occurs.
Required. Specifies the number of months between each set of occurrences.
Required. Specifies on which instance of the allowed days specified in daysOfWeek the maintenance occurs.
Optional. For schedules like: 'recur every month on the first Monday' or 'recur every 3 months on last Friday'.
Required. Specifies on which day of the week the maintenance occurs.
Required. Specifies the number of weeks between each set of occurrences.
Optional. For schedules like: 'recur every Monday' or 'recur every 3 weeks on Wednesday'.
Required. Recurrence schedule for the maintenance window.

Optional. The date the maintenance window activates.
If the current date is before this date, the maintenance window is inactive and will not be used for upgrades.
If not specified, the maintenance window will be active right away.

Required. The start time of the maintenance window. Accepted values are from '00:00' to '23:59'.
'utcOffset' applies to this field. For example: '02:00' with 'utcOffset: +02:00' means UTC time '00:00'.

Optional. The UTC offset in format +/-HH:mm. For example, '+05:30' for IST and '-07:00' for PST.
If not specified, the default is '+00:00'.
Maintenance window for the maintenance configuration.
Time slots on which upgrade is not allowed.
Required. The day of the week.

Required. Each integer hour represents a time range beginning at 0m after the hour ending at the next hour (non-inclusive).
0 corresponds to 00:00 UTC, 23 corresponds to 23:00 UTC. Specifying [0, 1] means the 00:00 - 02:00 UTC time range.
If two array entries specify the same day of the week, the applied configuration is the union of times in both entries.
Required. The name of the agent pool.
Optional. The availability zones of the agent pool.
Optional. AKS will associate the specified agent pool with the Capacity Reservation Group.
Optional. The number of agents (VMs) to host docker containers. Allowed values must be in the range of 1 to 100 (inclusive).
Optional. The source resource ID to create the agent pool from.
Optional. Whether to enable auto-scaling for the agent pool.
Optional. Whether to enable encryption at host for the agent pool.
Optional. Whether to enable FIPS for the agent pool.
Optional. Whether to enable node public IP for the agent pool.
Optional. Whether to enable Ultra SSD for the agent pool.
Optional. Profile specific to a managed agent pool in Gateway mode.
Optional. The GPU instance profile of the agent pool.
Optional. GPU settings.
Optional. The resource ID of the host group.
Optional. Kubelet configuration on agent pool nodes.
Optional. The kubelet disk type of the agent pool.
Optional. Linux OS configuration.
Optional. Local DNS configuration.
Optional. The maximum number of agents (VMs) to host docker containers. Allowed values must be in the range of 1 to 100 (inclusive).
Optional. The minimum number of agents (VMs) to host docker containers. Allowed values must be in the range of 1 to 100 (inclusive).
Optional. The maximum number of pods that can run on a node.
Optional. A message of the day for agent pool nodes.
Optional. The mode of the agent pool.
Optional. Network profile for the agent pool.
Optional. The node labels of the agent pool.
Optional. The node public IP prefix ID of the agent pool.
Optional. The node taints of the agent pool.
Optional. The Kubernetes version of the agent pool.
Optional. The OS disk size in GB of the agent pool.
Optional. The OS disk type of the agent pool.
Optional. The OS SKU of the agent pool.
Optional. The OS type of the agent pool.
Optional. Pod IP allocation mode.
Optional. The pod subnet ID of the agent pool.
Optional. Power State of the agent pool.
Optional. The proximity placement group resource ID of the agent pool.
Optional. The scale down mode of the agent pool.
Optional. The scale set eviction policy of the agent pool.
Optional. The scale set priority of the agent pool.
Optional. The security settings of the agent pool.
Optional. The spot max price of the agent pool.
Optional. The tags of the agent pool.
Optional. The type of the agent pool.
Optional. Upgrade settings for the agent pool.
Optional. The VM size of the agent pool.
Optional. Virtual Machines resource status.
Optional. The VNet subnet ID of the agent pool.
Optional. Windows OS configuration.
Optional. The workload runtime of the agent pool.
Optional. The name of diagnostic setting.
Optional. Name of a Diagnostic Log category for a resource type this setting is applied to. Set the specific logs to collect here.
Optional. Name of a Diagnostic Log category group for a resource type this setting is applied to.
Optional. Enable or disable the category explicitly. Default is `true`.
Optional. The name of logs that will be streamed ("allLogs" is not supported, see module documentation for the list of supported). Set to `[]` to disable log collection.
Required. Name of a Diagnostic Metric category for a resource type this setting is applied to. Set to `AllMetrics` to collect all metrics.
Optional. Enable or disable the category explicitly. Default is `true`.
Optional. The name of metrics that will be streamed. "allMetrics" includes all possible metrics for the resource. Set to `[]` to disable metric collection.
Optional. A string indicating whether the export to Log Analytics should use the default destination type, i.e. AzureDiagnostics, or use a destination type.

Optional. Resource ID of the diagnostic log analytics workspace. For security reasons, it is recommended to set diagnostic settings to send data to either storage account, log analytics workspace or event hub.

Optional. Resource ID of the diagnostic storage account. For security reasons, it is recommended to set diagnostic settings to send data to either storage account, log analytics workspace or event hub.
Optional. Resource ID of the diagnostic event hub authorization rule for the Event Hubs namespace in which the event hub should be created or streamed to.

Optional. Name of the diagnostic event hub within the namespace to which logs are streamed. Without this, an event hub is created for each log category. For security reasons, it is recommended to set diagnostic settings to send data to either storage account, log analytics workspace or event hub.
Optional. The full ARM resource ID of the Marketplace resource to which you would like to send Diagnostic Logs.
Optional. Specify the name of lock.
Optional. Specify the type of lock.
Required. Maintenance window for the maintenance configuration.
Optional. Time slots on which upgrade is not allowed.
Optional. If two array entries specify the same day of the week, the applied configuration is the union of times in both entries.
Optional. Enables system assigned managed identity on the resource.
Optional. The resource ID(s) to assign to the resource.

Required. The role to assign. You can provide either the display name of the role definition, the role definition GUID, or its fully qualified ID in the following format: '/providers/Microsoft.Authorization/roleDefinitions/c2f4ef07-c644-48eb-af81-4b1b4947fb11'.
Required. The principal ID of the principal (user/group/identity) to assign the role to.
Optional. The principal type of the assigned principal ID.
Optional. The description of the role assignment.

Optional. The conditions on the role assignment. This limits the resources it can be assigned to. e.g.: @Resource[Microsoft.Storage/storageAccounts/blobServices/containers:ContainerName] StringEqualsIgnoreCase "foo_storage_container".
Optional. Version of the condition.
Optional. The Resource Id of the delegated managed identity resource.
Required. Specifies the name of the AKS cluster.
Optional. Specifies the location of AKS cluster. It picks up Resource Group's location by default.
Optional. Name of the resource group containing agent pool nodes.
Optional. Specifies the DNS prefix specified when creating the managed cluster.

Optional. The managed identity definition for this resource. Only one type of identity is supported: system-assigned or user-assigned, but not both.

[Policy: drcp-aks-03] A managed identity must be used (not a service principal).
Optional. Tier of a managed cluster SKU.
Optional. The name of a managed cluster SKU.
Optional. The support plan for the Managed Cluster.

Optional. Version of Kubernetes specified when creating the managed cluster.
By default the latest LTS supported version will be used.

[Policy: drcp-aks-12] Setting this parameter to a version that is no longer supported by Microsoft will make the resource non-compliant.

Optional. Whether to enable Kubernetes Role-Based Access Control. Default: true.

[Policy: drcp-aks-03] Setting this parameter to 'false' will make the resource non-compliant.

Optional. If set to true, getting static credentials will be disabled for this cluster.
This must only be used on Managed Clusters that are AAD enabled. Default: true.

[Policy: drcp-aks-03] Setting this parameter to 'false' will make the resource non-compliant.

Optional. The Entra ID (Azure Active Directory) integration configuration.

The default is:
- enableAzureRBAC: value of 'enableRBAC' parameter
- managed: true
- tenantID: subscription().tenantId

[Policy: drcp-aks-03] Compliant use of this resource requires certain parameter values:
- enableAzureRBAC: true
- managed: true
- tenantID: current tenant

Optional. The access profile for managed cluster API server.
The default is:
- disableRunCommand: true
- enablePrivateCluster: true
- enablePrivateClusterPublicFQDN: true
// - enableVnetIntegration: false
- privateDNSZone: 'none'

[Policy: drcp-aks-02] enablePrivateCluster must be true.
[Policy: drcp-aks-16] disableRunCommand must be true.

Optional. Allow or deny public network access for AKS. Default: Disabled.

[Policy: drcp-aks-02] Setting this parameter to any value other than 'Disabled' will make the resource non-compliant.
Optional. Advanced Networking profile for enabling observability and security feature suite on a cluster. For more information see https://aka.ms/aksadvancednetworking.
Optional. The IP families used for the cluster.
Optional. NAT Gateway profile for the cluster.
Optional. Network mode used for building the Kubernetes network.
Optional. Specifies the network plugin used for building Kubernetes network. Default: azure

Optional. Mode of Azure CNI network plugin for building the Kubernetes network. Default: 'overlay'.

Not used for 'kubenet' network plugin.

See https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/aks/concepts-network-cni-overview for more information.
Optional. Specifies the network policy used for building Kubernetes network. Default: none.
Optional. Specifies the CIDR notation IP range from which to assign pod IPs when kubenet is used.

Optional. The CIDR notation IP ranges from which to assign pod IPs. One IPv4 CIDR is expected for single-stack networking. Two CIDRs, one for each IP family (IPv4/IPv6), is expected for dual-stack networking.
Optional. A CIDR notation IP range from which to assign service cluster IPs. It must not overlap with any Subnet IP ranges.

Optional. The CIDR notation IP ranges from which to assign service cluster IPs. One IPv4 CIDR is expected for single-stack networking. Two CIDRs, one for each IP family (IPv4/IPv6), is expected for dual-stack networking. They must not overlap with any Subnet IP ranges.

Optional. Specifies the IP address assigned to the Kubernetes DNS service.
It must be within the Kubernetes service address range specified in serviceCidr.
Optional. Static egress gateway profile for the cluster.
Optional. Network data plane used in the Kubernetes cluster. Not compatible with 'kubenet' network plugin.

Optional. Specifies the sku of the load balancer used by the virtual machine scale sets used by nodepools. The default is 'standard'.

See Azure Load Balancer SKUs (https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/load-balancer/skus) for more information about the differences between load balancer SKUs.
Optional. Configuration of the load balancer used by the virtual machine scale sets used by nodepools.

Optional. Specifies outbound (egress) routing method. Default is 'userDefinedRouting'.

[Policy: drcp-aks-02] Setting this parameter to 'loadBalancer' will make the resource non-compliant.

Setting outboundType requires AKS clusters with a vm-set-type of VirtualMachineScaleSets and load-balancer-sku of Standard.

For more information see https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/aks/egress-outboundtype
Optional. Whether the AzureBlob CSI Driver for the storage profile is enabled.
Optional. Whether the AzureDisk CSI Driver for the storage profile is enabled.
Optional. Whether the AzureFile CSI Driver for the storage profile is enabled.
Optional. Whether the snapshot controller for the storage profile is enabled.
Optional. Node provisioning settings that apply to the whole cluster.
Optional. The node resource group configuration profile.
Conditional. When using defaults in 'primaryAgentPoolProfile', provide here the Subnet resource ID for the primary agent pool in case of using a custom Virtual Network.

Optional. Properties of the primary agent pool.

[Policy: drcp-aks-02] enableNodePublicIP must be false (or absent) on all agent pools.

Optional. Define one or more secondary/additional agent pools.

[Policy: drcp-aks-02] enableNodePublicIP must be false (or absent) on all agent pools.
Optional. Specifies the administrator username of Linux virtual machines.
Optional. Specifies the SSH RSA public key string for the Linux nodes.
Optional. The auto upgrade configuration. By default it is set to update the node image to the stable (N-1) version.
Optional. Configuring AKS automatic upgrades schedules.
Optional. Specifies whether the httpApplicationRouting add-on is enabled or not.
Optional. Specifies whether the webApplicationRoutingEnabled add-on is enabled or not.
Optional. Specifies the resource ID of connected DNS zone. It will be ignored if `webApplicationRoutingEnabled` is set to `false`.
Optional. Ingress type for the default NginxIngressController custom resource. It will be ignored if `webApplicationRoutingEnabled` is set to `false`.

Optional. Specifies whether assign the DNS zone contributor role to the cluster service principal. It will be ignored if `webApplicationRoutingEnabled` is set to `false` or `dnsZoneResourceId` not provided.
Optional. Specifies whether the ingressApplicationGateway (AGIC) add-on is enabled or not.
Conditional. Specifies the resource ID of connected application gateway. Required if `ingressApplicationGatewayEnabled` is set to `true`.
Optional. Specifies whether the aciConnectorLinux add-on is enabled or not.

Optional. The pod identity profile of the Managed Cluster. See [use AAD pod identity](https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/aks/use-azure-ad-pod-identity) for more details on AAD pod identity integration.
Optional. Identities associated with the cluster.

Optional. Security profile for the managed cluster. When provided, this overrides the individual security params (securityProfileDefender, securityProfileAzureKeyVaultKms, imageCleaner, enableWorkloadIdentity).
Optional. Whether the The OIDC issuer profile of the Managed Cluster is enabled.
Optional. Whether to enable Workload Identity. Requires OIDC issuer profile to be enabled.

Optional. Specifies the configuration of Azure Policy add-on.
Default is enabled=true and config.version=v2.

[Policy: drcp-aks-11] Compliant use of this resource requires 'enabled' to be true.

securityProfileDefender.defaultValue: [if(not(empty(parameters('logAnalyticsWorkspaceResourceId'))), createObject('securityMonitoring', createObject('enabled', true()), 'logAnalyticsWorkspaceResourceId', parameters('logAnalyticsWorkspaceResourceId')), createObject('securityMonitoring', createObject('enabled', false())))]

metadata.description:
Optional. Azure Defender profile settings. By default the securityMonitoring.enabled is true

[Policy: drcp-aks-18] Setting securityMonitoring.enabled to a value other than 'true' will make this resource non-compliant.
Optional. Azure Key Vault key management service settings for the security profile.

Optional. Whether to enable Image Cleaner for Kubernetes.
By default it is set to 'enabled' wth 168 hours interval.

Conditional. Specifies whether the cost analysis add-on is enabled or not. Default is false.

If Enabled `enableStorageProfileDiskCSIDriver` is set to true as it is needed.
Optional. Specifies whether the OMS agent is enabled.

Optional. The diagnostic settings of the service.

Compliant use of this resource requires diagnostic settings to be configured:
- workspaceResourceId
- logCategoriesAndGroups to include the following categories:
  - 'kube-audit'
  - 'kube-audit-admin'
  - 'kube-apiserver'
  - 'kube-controller-manager'
  - 'kube-scheduler'

Conditional. Resource ID of the monitoring log analytics workspace.
Required if: Azure Defender (securityProfile.defender), addon OmsAgent (addonProfiles.omsagent), or azureMonitor is used

Not used for: diagnostic settings.

Optional. Configures Azure Monitor profile, including Container Insights, Prometheus and Graphana.

By default, Container Insights are enabled to the Log Analytics workspace specified in logAnalyticsWorkspaceResourceId.

Optional. Specifies the configuration for Azure Key Vault secrets provider add-on.
Default is enabled=true and config.enableSecretRotation=true.
Optional. Enable/Disable usage telemetry for module.
Optional. Array of role assignments to create.
Optional. The lock settings of the service.
Optional. Tags of the resource.
Optional. The Resource ID of the disk encryption set to use for enabling encryption at rest. For security reasons, this value should be provided.
Optional. Configurations for provisioning the cluster with HTTP proxy servers.

Optional. Workload Auto-scaler profile for the managed cluster.
Default enables KEDA for event-driven autoscaling.
Optional. Service mesh profile for a managed cluster.
Optional. AI toolchain operator settings that apply to the whole cluster.
Optional. Profile of the cluster bootstrap configuration.
Optional. The FQDN subdomain of the private cluster with custom private dns zone. This cannot be updated once the Managed Cluster has been created.
Optional. Settings for upgrading the cluster with override options.
Optional. The profile for Windows VMs in the Managed Cluster.
Optional. Parameters to be applied to the cluster-autoscaler when enabled.
Conditional. Information about a service principal identity for the cluster to use for manipulating Azure APIs. Required if no managed identities are assigned to the cluster.
Optional. Specifies whether the openServiceMesh add-on is enabled or not.
Optional. Specifies whether the kubeDashboard add-on is enabled or not.
Optional. Specifies whether the OMS agent is using managed identity authentication.

variables.formattedUserAssignedIdentities: [reduce(map(coalesce(tryGet(parameters('managedIdentities'), 'userAssignedResourceIds'), createArray()), lambda('id', createObject(format('{0}', lambdaVariables('id')), createObject()))), createObject(), lambda('cur', 'next', union(lambdaVariables('cur'), lambdaVariables('next'))))]
variables.identity: [if(not(empty(parameters('managedIdentities'))), createObject('type', if(coalesce(tryGet(parameters('managedIdentities'), 'systemAssigned'), false()), 'SystemAssigned', if(not(empty(coalesce(tryGet(parameters('managedIdentities'), 'userAssignedResourceIds'), createObject()))), 'UserAssigned', null())), 'userAssignedIdentities', if(not(empty(variables('formattedUserAssignedIdentities'))), variables('formattedUserAssignedIdentities'), null())), null())]

properties.securityProfile: [coalesce(parameters('securityProfile'), createObject('defender', if(coalesce(tryGet(tryGet(parameters('securityProfileDefender'), 'securityMonitoring'), 'enabled'), not(empty(tryGet(parameters('securityProfileDefender'), 'logAnalyticsWorkspaceResourceId')))), createObject('securityMonitoring', createObject('enabled', coalesce(tryGet(tryGet(parameters('securityProfileDefender'), 'securityMonitoring'), 'enabled'), true())), 'logAnalyticsWorkspaceResourceId', coalesce(tryGet(parameters('securityProfileDefender'), 'logAnalyticsWorkspaceResourceId'), parameters('logAnalyticsWorkspaceResourceId'))), null()), 'workloadIdentity', if(parameters('enableWorkloadIdentity'), createObject('enabled', parameters('enableWorkloadIdentity')), null()), 'imageCleaner', parameters('imageCleaner'), 'azureKeyVaultKms', if(not(empty(parameters('securityProfileAzureKeyVaultKms'))), createObject('enabled', coalesce(tryGet(parameters('securityProfileAzureKeyVaultKms'), 'enabled'), true()), 'keyVaultNetworkAccess', coalesce(tryGet(parameters('securityProfileAzureKeyVaultKms'), 'keyVaultNetworkAccess'), 'Private'), 'keyVaultResourceId', if(equals(coalesce(tryGet(parameters('securityProfileAzureKeyVaultKms'), 'keyVaultNetworkAccess'), 'Private'), 'Private'), tryGet(parameters('securityProfileAzureKeyVaultKms'), 'keyVaultResourceId'), null()), 'keyId', if(coalesce(tryGet(parameters('securityProfileAzureKeyVaultKms'), 'enabled'), true()), tryGet(parameters('securityProfileAzureKeyVaultKms'), 'keyId'), null())), null())))]
properties.linuxProfile: [if(not(empty(parameters('sshPublicKey'))), createObject('adminUsername', parameters('adminUsername'), 'ssh', createObject('publicKeys', createArray(createObject('keyData', coalesce(parameters('sshPublicKey'), ''))))), null())]

properties.notes: [if(equals(tryGet(parameters('lock'), 'kind'), 'CanNotDelete'), 'Cannot delete resource or child resources.', 'Cannot delete or modify the resource or child resources.')]

metadata.name: Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS) Managed Cluster Maintenance Configurations
metadata.description: This module deploys an Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS) Managed Cluster Maintenance Configurations.
Required. Maintenance window for the maintenance configuration.
Optional. Time slots on which upgrade is not allowed.
Optional. If two array entries specify the same day of the week, the applied configuration is the union of times in both entries.
Required. Length of maintenance window range from 4 to 24 hours.
The start date of the date span.
The end date of the date span.

Optional. Date ranges on which upgrade is not allowed. 'utcOffset' applies to this field.
For example, with 'utcOffset: +02:00' and 'dateSpan' being '2022-12-23' to '2023-01-03',
maintenance will be blocked from '2022-12-22 22:00' to '2023-01-03 22:00' in UTC time.
Required. The date of the month.
Required. Specifies the number of months between each set of occurrences.
Optional. For schedules like: 'recur every month on the 15th' or 'recur every 3 months on the 20th'.
Required. Specifies the number of days between each set of occurrences.
Optional. For schedules like: 'recur every day' or 'recur every 3 days'.
Required. Specifies on which day of the week the maintenance occurs.
Required. Specifies the number of months between each set of occurrences.
Required. Specifies on which instance of the allowed days specified in daysOfWeek the maintenance occurs.
Optional. For schedules like: 'recur every month on the first Monday' or 'recur every 3 months on last Friday'.
Required. Specifies on which day of the week the maintenance occurs.
Required. Specifies the number of weeks between each set of occurrences.
Optional. For schedules like: 'recur every Monday' or 'recur every 3 weeks on Wednesday'.
Required. Recurrence schedule for the maintenance window.

Optional. The date the maintenance window activates.
If the current date is before this date, the maintenance window is inactive and will not be used for upgrades.
If not specified, the maintenance window will be active right away.

Required. The start time of the maintenance window. Accepted values are from '00:00' to '23:59'.
'utcOffset' applies to this field. For example: '02:00' with 'utcOffset: +02:00' means UTC time '00:00'.

Optional. The UTC offset in format +/-HH:mm. For example, '+05:30' for IST and '-07:00' for PST.
If not specified, the default is '+00:00'.
Maintenance window for the maintenance configuration.
Time slots on which upgrade is not allowed.
Required. The day of the week.

Required. Each integer hour represents a time range beginning at 0m after the hour ending at the next hour (non-inclusive).
0 corresponds to 00:00 UTC, 23 corresponds to 23:00 UTC. Specifying [0, 1] means the 00:00 - 02:00 UTC time range.
If two array entries specify the same day of the week, the applied configuration is the union of times in both entries.
Required. Maintenance window for the maintenance configuration.
Optional. Time slots on which upgrade is not allowed.
Optional. If two array entries specify the same day of the week, the applied configuration is the union of times in both entries.
Conditional. The name of the parent managed cluster. Required if the template is used in a standalone deployment.
Optional. Name of the maintenance configuration.
The name of the maintenance configuration.
The resource ID of the maintenance configuration.
The resource group the agent pool was deployed into.

metadata.name: Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS) Managed Cluster Agent Pools
metadata.description: This module deploys an Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS) Managed Cluster Agent Pool.
Required. The name of the agent pool.
Optional. The availability zones of the agent pool.
Optional. AKS will associate the specified agent pool with the Capacity Reservation Group.
Optional. The number of agents (VMs) to host docker containers. Allowed values must be in the range of 1 to 100 (inclusive).
Optional. The source resource ID to create the agent pool from.
Optional. Whether to enable auto-scaling for the agent pool.
Optional. Whether to enable encryption at host for the agent pool.
Optional. Whether to enable FIPS for the agent pool.
Optional. Whether to enable node public IP for the agent pool.
Optional. Whether to enable Ultra SSD for the agent pool.
Optional. Profile specific to a managed agent pool in Gateway mode.
Optional. The GPU instance profile of the agent pool.
Optional. GPU settings.
Optional. The resource ID of the host group.
Optional. Kubelet configuration on agent pool nodes.
Optional. The kubelet disk type of the agent pool.
Optional. Linux OS configuration.
Optional. Local DNS configuration.
Optional. The maximum number of agents (VMs) to host docker containers. Allowed values must be in the range of 1 to 100 (inclusive).
Optional. The minimum number of agents (VMs) to host docker containers. Allowed values must be in the range of 1 to 100 (inclusive).
Optional. The maximum number of pods that can run on a node.
Optional. A message of the day for agent pool nodes.
Optional. The mode of the agent pool.
Optional. Network profile for the agent pool.
Optional. The node labels of the agent pool.
Optional. The node public IP prefix ID of the agent pool.
Optional. The node taints of the agent pool.
Optional. The Kubernetes version of the agent pool.
Optional. The OS disk size in GB of the agent pool.
Optional. The OS disk type of the agent pool.
Optional. The OS SKU of the agent pool.
Optional. The OS type of the agent pool.
Optional. Pod IP allocation mode.
Optional. The pod subnet ID of the agent pool.
Optional. Power State of the agent pool.
Optional. The proximity placement group resource ID of the agent pool.
Optional. The scale down mode of the agent pool.
Optional. The scale set eviction policy of the agent pool.
Optional. The scale set priority of the agent pool.
Optional. The security settings of the agent pool.
Optional. The spot max price of the agent pool.
Optional. The tags of the agent pool.
Optional. The type of the agent pool.
Optional. Upgrade settings for the agent pool.
Optional. The VM size of the agent pool.
Optional. Virtual Machines resource status.
Optional. The VNet subnet ID of the agent pool.
Optional. Windows OS configuration.
Optional. The workload runtime of the agent pool.
Conditional. The name of the parent managed cluster. Required if the template is used in a standalone deployment.
Required. Name of the agent pool.
Optional. The list of Availability zones to use for nodes. This can only be specified if the AgentPoolType property is "VirtualMachineScaleSets".
Optional. AKS will associate the specified agent pool with the Capacity Reservation Group.

Optional. Desired Number of agents (VMs) specified to host docker containers. Allowed values must be in the range of 0 to 1000 (inclusive) for user pools and in the range of 1 to 1000 (inclusive) for system pools. The default value is 1.
Optional. This is the ARM ID of the source object to be used to create the target object.
Optional. Whether to enable auto-scaler.
Optional. This is only supported on certain VM sizes and in certain Azure regions. For more information, see: /azure/aks/enable-host-encryption. For security reasons, this setting should be enabled.
Optional. See Add a FIPS-enabled node pool (https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/aks/use-multiple-node-pools#add-a-fips-enabled-node-pool-preview) for more details.

Optional. Some scenarios may require nodes in a node pool to receive their own dedicated public IP addresses. A common scenario is for gaming workloads, where a console needs to make a direct connection to a cloud virtual machine to minimize hops. For more information see assigning a public IP per node (https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/aks/use-multiple-node-pools#assign-a-public-ip-per-node-for-your-node-pools).
Optional. Whether to enable UltraSSD.
Optional. Profile specific to a managed agent pool in Gateway mode. Ignored if agent pool mode is not Gateway.
Optional. GPUInstanceProfile to be used to specify GPU MIG instance profile for supported GPU VM SKU.
Optional. GPU settings.

Optional. This is of the form /subscriptions/{subscriptionId}/resourcegroups/{resourcegroupname}/providers/microsoft.compute/hostgroups/{hostgroupname}. For more information see Azure Dedicated Hosts.
Optional. Kubelet configuration on agent pool nodes.
Optional. Determines the placement of emptyDir volumes, container runtime data root, and Kubelet ephemeral storage.
Optional. Linux OS configuration.
Optional. Local DNS configuration.
Optional. The maximum number of nodes for auto-scaling.
Optional. The maximum number of pods that can run on a node.
Optional. A message of the day will be a multi-line message that is prepended to the command prompt and the SSH login message. You can use escape characters like \n for new line.
Optional. The minimum number of nodes for auto-scaling.
Optional. A cluster must have at least one "System" Agent Pool at all times. For additional information on agent pool restrictions and best practices, see: /azure/aks/use-system-pools.
Optional. Network profile to be used for agent pool nodes.
Optional. The node labels to be persisted across all nodes in agent pool.
Optional. ResourceId of the node PublicIPPrefix.
Optional. The taints added to new nodes during node pool create and scale. For example, key=value:NoSchedule.

Optional. As a best practice, you should upgrade all node pools in an AKS cluster to the same Kubernetes version. The node pool version must have the same major version as the control plane. The node pool minor version must be within two minor versions of the control plane version. The node pool version cannot be greater than the control plane version. For more information see upgrading a node pool (https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/aks/use-multiple-node-pools#upgrade-a-node-pool).
Optional. OS Disk Size in GB to be used to specify the disk size for every machine in the master/agent pool. If you specify 0, it will apply the default osDisk size according to the vmSize specified.

Optional. The default is "Ephemeral" if the VM supports it and has a cache disk larger than the requested OSDiskSizeGB. Otherwise, defaults to "Managed". May not be changed after creation. For more information see Ephemeral OS (https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/aks/cluster-configuration#ephemeral-os).

Optional. Specifies the OS SKU used by the agent pool. The default is Ubuntu if OSType is Linux. The default is Windows2019 when Kubernetes <= 1.24 or Windows2022 when Kubernetes >= 1.25 if OSType is Windows.
Optional. The operating system type. The default is Linux.
Optional. Pod IP allocation mode.

Optional. Subnet ID for the pod IPs. If omitted, pod IPs are statically assigned on the node subnet (see vnetSubnetID for more details). This is of the form: /subscriptions/{subscriptionId}/resourceGroups/{resourceGroupName}/providers/Microsoft.Network/virtualNetworks/{virtualNetworkName}/subnets/{subnetName}.
Optional. Power State of the agent pool.
Optional. The ID for the Proximity Placement Group.
Optional. Describes how VMs are added to or removed from Agent Pools. See [billing states](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-machines/states-billing).
Optional. The eviction policy specifies what to do with the VM when it is evicted. The default is Delete. For more information about eviction see spot VMs.
Optional. The Virtual Machine Scale Set priority.
Optional. The security settings of an agent pool.

Optional. Possible values are any decimal value greater than zero or -1 which indicates the willingness to pay any on-demand price. For more details on spot pricing, see spot VMs pricing (https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-machines/spot-vms#pricing).
Optional. Tags of the resource.
Optional. The type of Agent Pool.
Optional. Upgrade settings for the agent pool.

Optional. VM size. VM size availability varies by region. If a node contains insufficient compute resources (memory, cpu, etc) pods might fail to run correctly. For more details on restricted VM sizes, see: /azure/aks/quotas-skus-regions.
Optional. Virtual Machines resource status.

Optional. Node Subnet ID. If this is not specified, a VNET and subnet will be generated and used. If no podSubnetID is specified, this applies to nodes and pods, otherwise it applies to just nodes. This is of the form: /subscriptions/{subscriptionId}/resourceGroups/{resourceGroupName}/providers/Microsoft.Network/virtualNetworks/{virtualNetworkName}/subnets/{subnetName}.
Optional. Windows OS configuration.
Optional. Determines the type of workload a node can run.
The name of the agent pool.
The resource ID of the agent pool.
The resource group the agent pool was deployed into.
The resource ID of the managed cluster.
The resource group the managed cluster was deployed into.
The name of the managed cluster.
The control plane FQDN of the managed cluster.
The principal ID of the system assigned identity.
The Client ID of the AKS identity.
The Object ID of the AKS identity.
The Resource ID of the AKS identity.
The Object ID of the OMS agent identity.
The Object ID of the Key Vault Secrets Provider identity.
The Client ID of the Key Vault Secrets Provider identity.
The Object ID of Application Gateway Ingress Controller (AGIC) identity.
The location the resource was deployed into.
The OIDC token issuer URL.
The addonProfiles of the Kubernetes cluster.
The Object ID of Web Application Routing.

Is there evidence of usage in non-compliance with policies? Returns 'true' if a non-compliance is identified.

Policies checked:
- [drcp-aks-02] publicNetworkAccess must be 'Disabled', enablePrivateCluster must be true, networkOutboundType must not be 'loadBalancer', enableNodePublicIP must be false on all agent pools
- [drcp-aks-03] disableLocalAccounts must be true, enableRBAC must be true, aadProfile.enableAzureRBAC must be true
- [drcp-aks-11] addonAzurePolicy.enabled must be true
- [drcp-aks-16] apiServerAccessProfile.disableRunCommand must be true
- [drcp-aks-18] securityProfileDefender.securityMonitoring.enabled must be true

Note: 'kubernetesVersion' (drcp-aks-12) is not analyzed as it depends on the supported version list at deployment time.

metadata.description: The list of diagnostic settings log categories that were configured to log analytics.
